jokes.text: TiVo jumping and freezing while Larry tries to demonstrate the problem
jokes.setup: Larry is trying to show the TiVo repair guy what's wrong
jokes.punchline: The device malfunctions on cue, perfectly demonstrating the problem
jokes.explanation: Classic situational comedy - the technology fails at the perfect moment to prove Larry's point. Well-structured visual gag.

jokes.text: Larry tells the guy not to spoil Lost, then the guy immediately spoils it anyway
jokes.setup: Larry explicitly asks not to be told about Lost plot developments
jokes.punchline: The repair guy ignores this completely and reveals major plot points
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of people not listening - the setup and immediate payoff work beautifully. Classic Larry David social awkwardness.

jokes.text: Larry juggling the panicked phone call from Cheryl while desperately trying to keep the TiVo guy there
jokes.setup: Larry finally has the hard-to-get repair guy at his house
jokes.punchline: His wife calls in distress from a plane during a storm, creating impossible competing priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's priorities and social awkwardness. The escalating tension between mundane and urgent is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Larry asking about warranty cards and installers while Cheryl is trying to tell him she might die
jokes.setup: Cheryl is having a life-threatening experience on the plane
jokes.punchline: Larry is completely focused on TiVo paperwork and technical details
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David character writing - his complete inability to prioritize human connection over minor inconveniences. The contrast is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Larry telling Cheryl to call back in 10 minutes while she's potentially facing death
jokes.setup: Cheryl is calling from a plane in severe turbulence, fearing for her life
jokes.punchline: Larry treats it like a scheduling inconvenience and asks her to call back later
jokes.explanation: This is the emotional center of the episode - Larry's complete tone-deafness reaches legendary levels. The dark comedy of asking someone to reschedule their potential death is masterfully executed.

jokes.text: Cheryl dramatically announcing 'I'm leaving' after the phone debacle
jokes.setup: The TiVo call has revealed Larry's priorities
jokes.punchline: Cheryl makes the ultimate relationship decision based on a repair appointment
jokes.explanation: The dramatic escalation from technical support call to marriage ending is both absurd and believable given Larry's character.

jokes.text: Larry's defense that he saved all her shows including 'Barefoot Contessa' and 'Project Runway'
jokes.setup: Cheryl is leaving him over his priorities
jokes.punchline: Larry thinks saving her TV shows should earn him relationship credit
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's complete misunderstanding of what matters in relationships - he thinks technical solutions equal emotional connection.

jokes.text: Revelation that Larry talks during sex, specifically about fake crab vs real crab
jokes.setup: Cheryl lists Larry's relationship failures
jokes.punchline: Larry discusses seafood authenticity during intimate moments
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor. The specificity of the crab topic makes it hilariously absurd while being completely believable for Larry.

jokes.text: Larry doubling down on the crab argument: 'You've gotta be a moron if you can't tell the difference'
jokes.setup: Cheryl has just revealed his inappropriate bedroom conversations
jokes.punchline: Instead of apologizing, Larry reinforces his position on crab identification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David moment - completely missing the point and defending an absurd position instead of addressing the real issue.

jokes.text: Cheryl's realization: 'There's no other side. This is it!'
jokes.setup: Cheryl has been defending Larry to others by claiming there's another side to him
jokes.punchline: She realizes the Larry everyone sees is actually all there is
jokes.explanation: Great character development joke - Cheryl's moment of clarity about Larry's personality being one-dimensional in the worst way.

jokes.text: Larry's immediate concern about the airplane guy's breath quality and mint availability
jokes.setup: Cheryl mentions having a meaningful conversation with another passenger
jokes.punchline: Larry focuses on whether the guy carries breath mints like he does
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's obsession with mundane preparedness details while missing the emotional point entirely.

jokes.text: The underwear company makes 'No-fly Zone' - underwear without flies
jokes.setup: Glenn owns an underwear company
jokes.punchline: It's for underwear specifically designed without the traditional fly opening
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about no-fly underwear that will pay off later in the episode. Absurd but specific product concept.

jokes.text: Larry's rant about how stupid no-fly underwear is: 'You have to drop the underwear to pee'
jokes.setup: Learning about Glenn's underwear business
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately identifies the obvious design flaw with passionate certainty
jokes.explanation: Larry's immediate, passionate critique of a product he's never used shows his tendency to have strong opinions about everything.

jokes.text: The tiny plate at Jeff and Susie's house
jokes.setup: Larry is being served food at Jeff's house
jokes.punchline: The plate is comically small, like something for a child or elderly person
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David observation about social situations - fixating on minor details while major drama unfolds.

jokes.text: Larry asking Cheryl to 'call me back in 10 minutes' when the plane is potentially going down
jokes.setup: Larry is recounting the phone call incident
jokes.punchline: The full absurdity becomes clear when retold to friends
jokes.explanation: The callback to the central premise gains power from being retold - Jeff and Susie's horrified reactions amplify the comedy.

jokes.text: Jeff's speech about loyalty to Larry: 'We have to stick with Larry' despite Cheryl being great
jokes.setup: The friend group is choosing sides in the breakup
jokes.punchline: Jeff makes a passionate loyalty speech about supporting Larry despite acknowledging he's wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing male loyalty logic - supporting your friend even when he's clearly in the wrong.

jokes.text: The dinner phone rule fight with Jeff physically wrestling Larry for the phone
jokes.setup: The phone rings during dinner and Larry wants to answer it
jokes.punchline: Jeff gets violent over his 'no phone during dinner' rule, leading to a physical altercation
jokes.explanation: Escalating physical comedy over an absurd principle - Jeff's dinner etiquette rule becomes more important than anything else.

jokes.text: Larry telling the restaurant hostess his wife left him to cancel reservation
jokes.setup: Larry needs to cancel a dinner reservation and is asked why
jokes.punchline: He overshares deeply personal information to a restaurant employee
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - inappropriate oversharing in a business context because he takes everything literally.

jokes.text: Iris's skeptical response and Larry's defensive escalation about the breakup story
jokes.setup: Larry has overshared about his marriage ending
jokes.punchline: He becomes increasingly defensive when he senses doubt from the hostess
jokes.explanation: Larry's paranoia about being believed leads him deeper into inappropriate territory with a restaurant worker.

jokes.text: Larry's sarcastic promise: 'In the future, if my wife leaves me, I'll make sure she does it in the morning'
jokes.setup: Iris asks for more notice on cancellations
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically suggests he'll schedule future marital breakdowns around restaurant policy
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcastic comeback that highlights the absurdity of applying business policies to personal crises. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Larry realizing Simon used the $10,000 loan to throw himself an expensive birthday party
jokes.setup: Larry sees the elaborate party invitation at Jeff's house
jokes.punchline: He realizes his loan money is funding Simon's own birthday celebration
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's justified indignation - the absurdity of borrowing money to throw yourself a party.

jokes.text: Larry asking Cheryl to call the restaurant to confirm their breakup story
jokes.setup: Larry is paranoid that the restaurant doesn't believe his cancellation excuse
jokes.punchline: He asks his estranged wife to verify their personal crisis to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry David absurdity - asking the person leaving you to verify to a business that they're leaving you. The callback structure is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Larry's logic: 'Who would go to the lengths of saying their wife split up with them?' / Cheryl: 'You would.'
jokes.setup: Larry argues no one would fake such an extreme excuse
jokes.punchline: Cheryl immediately identifies that Larry absolutely would do exactly that
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Cheryl knows Larry so well she immediately calls out his self-deceptive reasoning. Brilliant setup and payoff.

jokes.text: Simon disinviting Larry from his own party: 'We chose Cheryl'
jokes.setup: Larry is planning to attend Simon's birthday party
jokes.punchline: Simon uninvites the person who loaned him the money to fund the party
jokes.explanation: The ultimate insult - being disinvited from an event you're essentially paying for. Good escalation of the social choosing sides theme.

jokes.text: Larry accidentally ripping the Xena Warrior Princess picture at the dry cleaners
jokes.setup: Larry is looking at memorabilia while waiting for his clothes
jokes.punchline: He accidentally destroys the dry cleaner's prized Xena photograph
jokes.explanation: Setup for later Lucy Lawless encounter. Standard physical comedy but serves the plot structure well.

jokes.text: Larry being denied a table at Primo's despite the reservation because Iris doesn't believe his wife left him
jokes.setup: Larry returns to the restaurant that questioned his breakup excuse
jokes.punchline: His paranoia was justified - they're actually punishing him for the perceived lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - Larry's paranoia was actually justified and his worst fears are realized. Great escalation structure.

jokes.text: Restaurant hostess not believing breakup story, making Larry wait
jokes.setup: Larry's earlier phone call about his wife leaving
jokes.punchline: Hostess punishing him by making him wait, not believing the story
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation. Larry's paranoia about not being believed becomes self-fulfilling prophecy.

jokes.text: Man at restaurant loudly complaining about Larry talking to himself
jokes.setup: Larry talking to himself while man talks loudly on phone
jokes.punchline: Phone talker calling Larry annoying for self-conversation
jokes.explanation: Decent irony about social etiquette and hypocrisy but not exceptional

jokes.text: Larry arguing with the cell phone talker while talking to himself
jokes.setup: Larry is annoyed by someone talking loudly on their phone in the restaurant
jokes.punchline: The person points out Larry is talking to himself just as loudly
jokes.explanation: Great Larry David social observation - he complains about behavior he's engaging in himself. The hypocrisy is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: The Funkhousers choosing Cheryl over Larry
jokes.setup: Larry's social circle is taking sides in the breakup
jokes.punchline: Even more friends abandon him with matter-of-fact delivery
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of everyone choosing Cheryl. The deadpan delivery adds to Larry's isolation.

jokes.text: Funkhouser family choosing Cheryl over Larry
jokes.setup: Pattern of friends choosing sides in breakup
jokes.punchline: Even more friends abandon Larry for Cheryl
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern but without much new insight or humor

jokes.text: Alana the housekeeper also working for Cheryl now and choosing her over Larry
jokes.setup: Larry encounters his former housekeeper at Cheryl's sister's house
jokes.punchline: Even his domestic help has switched allegiances
jokes.explanation: Another escalation in Larry's social abandonment. The help choosing sides adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Larry's comment about Glenn's 'nice head of hair'
jokes.setup: Larry meets his wife's new romantic interest
jokes.punchline: His first comment is about the man's superior hair
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry observation - focusing on superficial details during emotionally charged moments. Shows his petty jealousy.

jokes.text: Larry's passionate defense of penis rights: 'The penis needs an option, just like everybody else. The penis is human.'
jokes.setup: Glenn explains his no-fly underwear business model
jokes.punchline: Larry anthropomorphizes male anatomy to make an underwear argument
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy - Larry's passionate advocacy for anatomical civil rights is both ridiculous and delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Larry offering Glenn mints, tissues, and a pen in sequence
jokes.setup: Larry has mentioned his preparedness with these items earlier
jokes.punchline: He systematically offers his pocket supplies to prove his superiority
jokes.explanation: Callback to Larry's earlier boasting about preparedness. Shows his petty competitiveness with Glenn.

jokes.text: Larry's indignation about losing to a guy with 'thick, presidential Kennedy hair'
jokes.setup: Larry is lamenting losing Cheryl to Glenn
jokes.punchline: He's most upset about the quality of his rival's hair
jokes.explanation: Larry's fixation on superficial advantages shows his vanity and misplaced priorities even in heartbreak.

jokes.text: Larry telling Lucy Lawless 'I actually think I could take you' after seeing her in person
jokes.setup: Larry admits he used to be intimidated by the Warrior Princess character
jokes.punchline: He now believes he could physically defeat the actress in combat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - inappropriate confidence and complete lack of social awareness in front of a potential date.

jokes.text: Larry's pickup line: 'I'm not a cool guy or anything... I would love to waste your time for a couple of hours'
jokes.setup: Larry is asking Lucy Lawless out on a date
jokes.punchline: He uses self-deprecation and honesty about wasting her time as his romantic approach
jokes.explanation: Great character writing - Larry's unconventional honesty accidentally becomes charming. The reverse psychology works despite itself.

jokes.text: Larry's excitement about his pickup line working: 'I'm not a cool guy'! I'm gonna use it all the time!'
jokes.setup: Larry successfully asked out Lucy Lawless
jokes.punchline: He immediately wants to systematize his accidental success into a dating strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character beat - turning a spontaneous moment into a calculated system, missing why it worked in the first place.

jokes.text: Ted Danson choosing Cheryl too and not inviting Larry to the ocean fundraiser
jokes.setup: Larry realizes Ted called about a fundraiser
jokes.punchline: Even Ted Danson has chosen Cheryl's side in the breakup
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of social abandonment. Even celebrity acquaintances are taking sides.

jokes.text: Larry's testicles getting caught in his underwear fly causing 'scrotal hematoma'
jokes.setup: Larry is getting dressed up for his date
jokes.punchline: His own underwear literally attacks him, proving Glenn's no-fly design right
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff - Larry's passionate opposition to no-fly underwear is violently proven wrong by his own anatomy. The physical comedy and irony are perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Doctor recommending No-fly Zone underwear after Larry's injury
jokes.setup: Larry has been injured by traditional underwear
jokes.punchline: Medical professional recommends the exact product Larry ridiculed
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure - the doctor's professional recommendation validates Glenn's business and Larry's need for the product he mocked.

jokes.text: Leon's diagnosis: 'You got long balls, Larry. Long balls.'
jokes.setup: The doctor explains Larry's unusual anatomy
jokes.punchline: Leon immediately coins a nickname based on the medical condition
jokes.explanation: Leon's immediate nickname creation is perfectly in character. The medical condition becomes a comedy runner.

jokes.text: Doctor telling Larry no sex for a week right before his big date
jokes.setup: Larry finally has a promising date after his marriage ended
jokes.punchline: Medical restrictions prevent any romantic possibilities
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David timing - medical intervention ruins his first post-divorce romantic opportunity.

jokes.text: Primo restaurant owner: 'We chose Cheryl'
jokes.setup: Larry tries to get a table at his usual restaurant
jokes.punchline: Even business establishments have taken sides in his personal life
jokes.explanation: Peak escalation of the choosing sides theme - even businesses are participating in the social exile. The economy of the delivery makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Larry's medical confession ruining the romantic moment with Lucy Lawless
jokes.setup: Lucy suggests going to her house, which Larry interprets sexually
jokes.punchline: He has to explain his genital injury and medical restrictions
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Larry's oversharing and assumptions destroy what could have been a simple dinner invitation. Multiple callbacks converge beautifully.

jokes.text: Larry explaining he can't have sex due to testicle injury when Lucy suggests her house
jokes.setup: Lucy suggesting they go to her house for 'dinner'
jokes.punchline: Larry assumes sex and explains his medical restriction
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry misunderstanding leading to awkward overshare

jokes.text: Lucy Lawless: 'I was only talking about dinner, you big asshole'
jokes.setup: Larry has just explained his sexual medical restrictions
jokes.punchline: She reveals she was never suggesting anything sexual, making his confession absurd
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection payoff - Larry's assumptions and oversharing were completely unnecessary. The 'big asshole' insult is perfectly earned and delivered.

jokes.text: Larry fighting the bouncer at Simon's party: 'That's my money!'
jokes.setup: Larry is denied entry to the party he's financing
jokes.punchline: He physically fights for access to an event funded by his loan
jokes.explanation: Good physical escalation and callback to the loan situation. Larry's righteous indignation leads to violence.

jokes.text: Larry's TiVo breaking down during the Lakers game ending
jokes.setup: Larry went through all the trouble to get his TiVo fixed
jokes.punchline: It malfunctions at the crucial moment of the game he was recording
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend callback - the TiVo that caused his marriage to end still doesn't work properly, making all his sacrifices pointless. Great circular structure.
